TO_CHAR(MA.MAX_LAST_REFRESH_TIME, :B12 )AS "last_refresh_time", MA.SUM_REFRESH_COUNT AS "refresh_count", MA.MAX_INST_ID AS "inst_id", MA.MAX_SESSION_ID A S "session_id", MA.MAX_SESSION_SERIAL AS "session_serial", MA.MAX_USERID AS "user_id", MA.MAX_USERNAME AS "user", MA.MAX_CON_ID AS "con_id", MA.MAX_CON_NAME AS "con_name", MA.MAX_MODULE AS "module", MA.MAX_ACTION AS "action", MA.MAX_SERVICE_NAME AS "service", MA.MAX_CLIENT_ID AS "client_id", MA.MAX_CLIENT_INFO AS "client_info", MA.MAX_PROGRAM AS "program", MA.MAX_PL_ENTRY_OID AS "plsql_entry_object_id", MA.MAX_PL_ENTRY_PROGID AS "plsql_entry_subprogram_id", MA.MAX_PL_OID AS "plsql_object_id", MA.MAX_PL_PROGID AS "plsql_subprogram_id", MA.MAX_PX_IS_CROSS_INSTANCE AS "is_cross_instance", MA.MAX_PX_DOP AS "dop", MA.MAX_PX_DOP_INSTANCES AS "instances"), CASE WHEN MA.MAX_ERROR_NUMBER IS NULL THEN NULL ELSE XMLELEMENT( "error", XMLATTRIBUTES(MA.MAX_ERROR_NUMBER AS "numbe r", MA.MAX_ERROR_FACILITY AS "facility"), MA.MAX_ERROR_MESSAGE) END, XMLELEMENT( "stats", XMLATTRIBUTES('monitor' AS "type"), XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('duration' AS "name"), GREATEST(ROUND((MA.MAX_LAST_REFRESH_TIME- NVL(MA.MAX_SQL_EXEC_START, MA.MIN_FIRST_REFRESH_TIME))*86400), LEAST(MA.SUM_ELAPSED_TIME/1000000, 1), CEIL(MA.MAX_QUEUING_TIME/1000000))), DECODE(MA.SUM_ELAPSED_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('elapsed_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_ELAPSED_TIME)), DECODE(MA.MAX_QUEUING_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('queuing_time' AS "name"), MA.MAX_QUEUING_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_CPU_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('cpu_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_CPU_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_USER_IO_WAIT_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('user_io_wait_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_ USER_IO_WAIT_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_APPLICATION_WAIT_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('application_wait_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_APPLICATION_WAIT_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_CONCURRENCY_WAIT_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('concurrency_wait_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_CONCURRENCY_WAIT_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_CLUSTER_WAIT_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('cluster_wait_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_CLUSTER_WAIT_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_PLSQL_EXEC_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('plsql_exec_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_PLSQL_EXEC_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_JAVA_EXEC_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('java_exec_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_JAVA_EXEC_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_OTHER_WAIT_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('other_wait_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_OTHER_WAIT_TIME)), DEC ODE(MA.SUM_FETCHES, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('user_fetch_count' AS "name"), MA.SUM_FETCHES)), DECODE(MA.SUM_BUFFER_GETS, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('buffer_gets' AS "name"), MA.SUM_BUFFER_GETS)), DECODE(MA.SUM_READ_REQS, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('read_reqs' AS "name"), MA.SUM_READ_REQS)), DECODE(MA.SUM_READ_BYTES, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('read_bytes' AS "name"), MA.SUM_READ_BYTES)), DECODE(MA.SUM_WRITE_REQS, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('write_reqs' AS "name"), MA.SUM_WRITE_REQS)), DECODE(MA.SUM_WRITE_BYTES, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('write_bytes' AS "name"), MA.SUM_WRITE_BYTES)), CASE WHEN MA.SUM_IO_INTER_BYTES IS NULL OR MA.SUM_IO_INTER_BYTES = MA.SUM_IO_BYTES OR MA.SUM_IO_BYTES = 0 OR NVL(MA.SUM_IO_ELIG_BYTES, 0) = 0 THEN NULL E LSE XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('cell_offload_efficiency' AS "name"), ROUND(MA.SUM_IO_BYTES / DECODE(MA.SUM_IO_INTER_BYTES, 0, 1, MA.SUM_IO_INTER_BYTES), 2)) END, CASE WHEN NVL(MA.SUM_IO_ELIG_BYTES, 0) = 0 OR MA.SUM_IO_UNC_BYTES = MA.SUM_IO_RET_BYTES THEN NULL ELSE XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('cell_offload_efficiency2' AS "name"), GREATEST(0, ROUND(100 * (MA.SUM_IO_UNC_BYTES - MA.SUM_IO_RET_BYTES) / MA.SUM_IO_ELIG_BYTES, 2))) END) ) ELSE XMLELEMENT( "sql", XMLATTRIBUTES( MA.MAX_SQL_ID AS "sql_id", TO_CHAR(MA.MAX_SQL_EXEC_START, :B12 ) AS "sql_exec_start", MA.MAX_SQL_EXEC_ID AS "sql_exec_id"), XMLFOREST(MA.MAX_STATUS AS "status", SUBSTR(MA.SQLMON_TEXT, 1, 100) AS "sql_text", TO_CHAR(MA.MIN_FIRST_REFRESH_TIME, :B12 ) AS "first_refresh_time", TO_CHAR(MA.MAX_LAST_REFRESH_TIME, :B12 ) AS "last_refresh_time", MA.SUM_REFRESH_COUNT AS "refresh_count", MA.MAX_INST_ID AS "inst_id", MA.MAX_SESSION_ID AS "session_id", MA.MAX_SESSION_SERIAL AS "session_serial", MA.MAX_USERID AS "user_id", MA.MAX_USERNAME AS "user", MA.MAX_CON_ID AS "con_id", MA.MAX_CON_NAME AS "con_name", MA.MAX_MODULE AS "module", MA.MAX_ACTION AS "action", MA.MAX_SERVICE_NAME AS "service", MA.MAX_CLIENT_ID AS "client_id", MA.MAX_CLIENT_INFO AS "client_info", MA.MAX_PROGRAM AS "program", MA.MAX_PLAN_HASH_VALUE AS "plan_hash", MA.MAX_PL_ENTRY_OID AS "plsql_entry_object_id", MA.MAX_PL_ENTRY_PROGID AS "plsql_entry_subprogram_id", MA.MAX_PL_OID AS "plsql_object_id", MA.MAX_PL_PROGID AS "plsql_subprogram_id", MA.MAX_PX_IS_CROSS_INSTANCE AS "is_cross_instance", MA.MAX_PX_DOP AS "dop", MA.MAX_PX_DOP_INSTANCES AS "instances" , MA.SUM_SERVERS_REQUESTED AS "px_servers_requested", MA.SUM_SERVERS_ALLOCATED AS "px_servers_allocated"), XMLELEMENT( "stats", XMLATTRIBUTES('monitor' AS "type"), XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('duration' AS "name"), GREATEST(ROUND((MA.MAX_LAST_REFRESH_TIME- MA.MAX_SQL_EXEC_START) * 86400), LEAST(MA.SUM_ELAPSED_TIME/1000000, 1), CEIL(MA.MAX_QUEUING_TIME/1000000))), DECODE(MA.SUM_ELAPSED_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('elapsed_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_ELAPSED_TIME)), DECODE(MA.MAX_QUEUING_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('queuing_time' AS "name"), MA.MAX_QUEUING_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_CPU_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('cpu_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_CPU_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_USER_IO_WAIT_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('user_io_wait_time' AS "name "), MA.SUM_USER_IO_WAIT_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_APPLICATION_WAIT_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('application_wait_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_APPLICATION_WAIT_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_CONCURRENCY_WAIT_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('concurrency_wait_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_CONCURRENCY_WAIT_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_CLUSTER_WAIT_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('cluster_wait_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_CLUSTER_WAIT_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_PLSQL_EXEC_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('plsql_exec_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_PLSQL_EXEC_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_JAVA_EXEC_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('java_exec_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_JAVA_EXEC_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_OTHER_WAIT_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('other_wait_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_OTHER_ WAIT_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_FETCHES, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('user_fetch_count' AS "name"), MA.SUM_FETCHES)), DECODE(MA.SUM_BUFFER_GETS, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('buffer_gets' AS "name"), MA.SUM_BUFFER_GETS)), DECODE(MA.SUM_READ_REQS, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('read_reqs' AS "name"), MA.SUM_READ_REQS)), DECODE(MA.SUM_READ_BYTES, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('read_bytes' AS "name"), MA.SUM_READ_BYTES)), DECODE(MA.SUM_WRITE_REQS, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('write_reqs' AS "name"), MA.SUM_WRITE_REQS)), DECODE(MA.SUM_WRITE_BYTES, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('write_bytes' AS "name"), MA.SUM_WRITE_BYTES)), DECODE(MA.SUM_IO_UNC_BYTES, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('unc_bytes' AS "name"), MA.SUM_IO_UNC_BYT ES)), DECODE(MA.SUM_IO_ELIG_BYTES, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('elig_bytes' AS "name"), MA.SUM_IO_ELIG_BYTES)), DECODE(MA.SUM_IO_RET_BYTES, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('ret_bytes' AS "name"), MA.SUM_IO_RET_BYTES)), CASE WHEN MA.SUM_IO_INTER_BYTES IS NULL OR MA.SUM_IO_INTER_BYTES = MA.SUM_IO_BYTES OR MA.SUM_IO_BYTES = 0 OR NVL(MA.SUM_IO_ELIG_BYTES, 0) = 0 THEN NULL ELSE XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('cell_offload_efficiency' AS "name"), ROUND(MA.SUM_IO_BYTES / DECODE(MA.SUM_IO_INTER_BYTES, 0, 1, MA.SUM_IO_INTER_BYTES), 2)) END, CASE WHEN NVL(MA.SUM_IO_ELIG_BYTES, 0) = 0 OR MA.SUM_IO_UNC_BYTES = MA.SUM_IO_RET_BYTES THEN NULL ELSE XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('cell_offload_efficiency2' AS "name"), GREATEST(0, ROUND(100 * (MA.SUM_IO_UNC_BYTES - MA.SUM_IO_RET_BYTES) / MA.SUM_IO_ELIG_BYTES, 2))) END) ) END) FROM MONITOR_AGG MA) ELSE NULL END) FROM ( SELECT CASE WHEN V1.XPLAN_XML IS NULL OR V1.XPLAN_XML.EXISTSNODE('/error') > 0 THEN NULL ELSE V1.XPLAN_XML END XPLAN_XML FROM (SELECT CASE WHEN :B36 = 1 THEN DBMS_XPLAN.BUILD_PLAN_XML( TABLE_NAME=>'gv$sql_plan', PLAN_TAG=>'plan', FILTER_PREDS=>:B35 , FORMAT=>'-PROJECTION +ALIAS +ADAPTIVE') ELSE NULL END XPLAN_XML FROM DUAL) V1) CONST_VIEW
20d3rm264s4yp select b.flag, b.taskId, b.lockerCode, b.lockerName, b.nodeId, b.complainType, b.complainTend, b.callNumber, count(tbr.business_id) relation, count(inboundno) phoneCount, b.business_id, b.business_name, b.business_status, b.temp_id, b.temp_version, b.process_id, b.process_version, b.process_instance_id, b.first_category_name, b.first_category_code, b.second_category_name, b.second_category_code, b.third_category_name, b.third_category_code, b.total_time, b.handle_code, b.handle_name, b.handle_date, b.handle_dept_code, b.handle_dept_name, b.urgent_level, b.if_reply, b.reply_time, b.press_num, b.hope_time, b.end_time, b.warning_flag, b.handle_position_code, b.handle_position_name, b.certificate_upload_status from ( select 2 as flag, t.ID_ as taskId , NVL(t.entrust_people_code, t.locker_code) as lockerCode, NVL(t.entrust_people_name, t.locker_name) as lockerName, t.TASK_DEF_KEY_ as nodeId , c.complainType, c.complainTend, c.callNumber, b.business_id, b.business_name, b.business_status, b.temp_id, b.temp_version, b.process_id, b.process_version, b.process_instance_id, b.first_category_name, b.first_category_code, b.second_category_name, b.second_category_code, b.third_category_name, b.third_category_code, b.total_time, b.handle_code, b.handle_name, b.handle_date, b.handle_dept_code, b.handle_dept_name, b.urgent_level, b.if_reply, b.reply_time, b.press_num, b.hope_time, b.end_time, b.warning_flag, b.handle_position_code, b.handle_position_name, b.certificate_upload_status from ACT_RU_TASK t left join tb_bpm_business b on t.PROC_INST_ID_ = b.process_instance_id left join ACT_RU_IDENTITYLINK i on i.TASK_ID_ = t.ID_ left join TB_BPM_BUSINESS_SEARCH c on b.business_id = c.id WHERE (t.locker_code is null or t.locker_code ='') and b.business_status = 1 and (t. task_status = 0 or t.task_status is null) and t.entrust_people_code is null and t.ASSIGNEE_ = :1 and b.handle_dept_code = :2 and (i.GROUP_ID_ is null or i.GROUP_ID_ ='') union select 3 as flag, t.ID_ as taskId , NVL(t.entrust_people_code, t.locker_code) as lockerCode, NVL(t.entrust_people_name, t.locker_name) as lockerName, t.TASK_DEF_KEY_ as nodeId, c.complainType, c.complainTend, c.callNumber, b.business_id, b.business_name, b.business_status, b.temp_id, b.temp_version, b.process_id, b.process_version, b.process_instance_id, b.first_category_name, b.first_category_code, b.second_category_name, b.second_category_code, b.third_category_name, b.third_category_code, b.total_time, b.handle_code, b.handle_name, b.handle_date, b.handle_dept_code, b.handle_dept_name, b.urgent_level, b.if_reply, b.reply_time, b.press_num, b.hope_time, b.end_time, b.warning_flag, b.handle_position_code, b.handle_position_name, b.certificate_upload_status from ACT_RU_TASK t left join tb_bpm_business b on t.PROC_INST_ID_ = b.process_instance_id left join ACT_RU_IDENTITYLINK i on i.TASK_ID_ = t.ID_ left join TB_BPM_BUSINESS_SEARCH c on b.business_id = c.id WHERE (t.locker_code is null or t.locker_code ='') and b.business_status = 1 and (t.task_status = 0 or t.task_status is null) and t.entrust_people_code is null and t.ASSIGNEE_ = :3 and i.GROUP_ID_ = :4 ) b left join tb_bpm_relate tbr on b.business_id = tbr.business_id left join tb_core_summary_record tcsr on b.callNumber = tcsr.inboundno and tcsr.calltype = '0' group by b.flag, b.taskId, b.lockerCode, b.lockerName, b.nodeId, b.complainType, b.complain Tend, b.callNumber, b.business_id, b.business_name, b.business_status, b.temp_id, b.temp_version, b.process_id, b.process_version, b.process_instance_id, b.first_category_name, b.first_category_code, b.second_category_name, b.second_category_code, b.third_category_name, b.third_category_code, b.total_time, b.handle_code, b.handle_name, b.handle_date, b.handle_dept_code, b.handle_dept_name, b.urgent_level, b.if_reply, b.reply_time, b.press_num, b.hope_time, b.end_time, b.warning_flag, b.handle_position_code, b.handle_position_name, b.certificate_upload_status order by (case when business_name = '??????' and complainType = '4' then '1' when business_name = '??????' and complainType != '4' then '2' else '3' end ) asc, (case when business_name = '????' and complainTend = '0' then '1' else '2' end ) asc, (case when business_name = '??????' then '1' else '2' end ) asc, case when relation >= 1 then '1' else '2' end asc, case when phoneCount >= 2 then '1' else '2' end asc
24bv1fq3ww614select count(id) from TB_CORE_BULLETIN where bulletin_handle_status = '1'
2ks9fgc224pndselect distinct tcr.role_code from tb_core_users m join tb_core_urmap tcu on tcu.user_id = m.id join tb_core_roles tcr on tcr.role_id = tcu.role_id WHERE m.job_num = :1 and tcr.role_code = :2 and USER_INTRANET is not null

34rznuxy8h2a4** SQL Text Not Available **
38243c4tqrkxm** SQL Text Not Available **
38fffx897xs0v** SQL Text Not Available **
38z2dr4ssxswn** SQL Text Not Available **
3du0852zx515qSELECT id, field_id, opt_value, opt_name FROM tb_bpm_field_opt WHERE (field_id = :1 )
3ms7w0c6ph91t** SQL Text Not Available **
3vvptt7gujqfmselect SEQ_GLOBAL_ID.Nextval from dual
3wnqv507rm4rySELECT flow_id, business_id, process_instance_id, flow_type, submit_task_id, target_task_id, opinion, submit_dept_code, submit_dept_name, submit_node_code, submit_node_name, copy_group_codes, copy_group_names, copy_people_codes, copy_people_names, target_code, target_name, target_node_code, target_node_name, target_dept_code, target_dept_name, joint_people_codes, joint_people_names, entrust_people_code, entrust_people_name, create_time, create_id, create_name FROM tb_bpm_flow WHERE (target_node_code = :1 AND process_instance_id = :2 AND flow_type IN (:3 , :4 , :5 ))
3wrrjm9qtr2my** SQL Text Not Available **
3zz0h6cr4wbyw** SQL Text Not Available **
45ub03q00bbj2 select * from(select rownum, t.id, t.fromjid, t.tojid, t.loginid, t.time, t.body, t.channel, t.messagetype, t.robotmsgtype, t.chatmsgtype, t.issensitive, t.cancelflag, t.messageid, t.robotid, t.file_id, t2.body quotebody, t2.fromjid quotefromjid, t2.file_id quotefileid, t2.chatmsgtype quotetype from tb_im_messageinfo t left join tb_im_messageinfo t2 on t.quotebody = t2.messageid where (t.fromjid = :1 or t.tojid = :2 ) and t.messageid not in (SELECT to_char(body) FROM tb_im_messageinfo b WHERE b.tojid = :3 AND b.CHATMSGTYPE =8 ) and t.chatmsgtype not in (3, 8) order by t.time desc, t.messageid desc) where rownum >= :4 and rownum <= :5

4rq0sjajsh0xh SELECT count(0) FROM (SELECT b.flag, b.taskId, b.lockerCode, b.lockerName, b.nodeId, b.complainType, b.complainTend, b.callNumber, count(tbr.business_id) relation, count(inboundno) phoneCount, b.business_id, b.business_name, b.business_status, b.temp_id, b.temp_version, b.process_id, b.process_version, b.process_instance_id, b.first_category_name, b.first_category_code, b.second_category_name, b.second_category_code, b.third_category_name, b.third_category_code, b.total_time, b.handle_code, b.handle_name, b.handle_date, b.handle_dept_code, b.handle_dept_name, b.urgent_level, b.if_reply, b.reply_time, b.press_num, b.hope_time, b.end_time, b.warning_flag, b.handle_position_code, b.handle_position_name, b.certificate_upload_status FROM (SELECT 1 AS flag, t.ID_ AS taskId, NVL(t.entrust_people_code, t.locker_code) AS lockerCode, NVL(t.entrust_people_name, t.locker_name) AS lockerName, t.TASK_DEF_KEY_ AS nodeId, c.complainType, c.complainTend, c.callNumber, b.business_id, b.business_name, b.business_status, b.temp_id, b.temp_version, b.process_id, b.process_version, b.process_instance_id, b.first_category_name, b.first_category_code, b.second_category_name, b.second_category_code, b.third_category_name, b.third_category_code, b.total_time, b.handle_code, b.handle_name, b.handle_date, b.handle_dept_code, b.handle_dept_name, b.urgent_level, b.if_reply, b.reply_time, b.press_num, b.hope_time, b.end_time, b.warning_flag, b.handle_position_code, b.handle_position_name, b.certificate_upload_status FROM ACT_RU_TASK t LEFT JOIN tb_bpm_business b ON t.PROC_INST_ID_ = b.process_instance_id LEFT JOIN TB_BPM_BUSINESS_SEARCH c ON b.business_id = c.id WHERE t.locker_code = :1 AND t.task_status = 1 AND b.business_status = 1 AND t.entrust_people_code IS NULL AND t.ASSIGNEE_ IS NULL UNION SELECT 4 AS flag, t.ID_ AS taskId, NVL(t.entrust_people_code, t.locker_code) AS lockerCode, NVL(t.entrust_people_name, t.locker_name) AS lockerName, t.TASK_DEF_KEY_ AS nodeId, c.complainType, c.complainTend, c.callNumber, b.business_id, b.business_name, b.business_status, b.temp_id, b.temp_version, b.process_id, b.process_version, b.process_instance_id, b.first_category_name, b.first_category_code, b.second_category_name, b.second_category_code, b.third_category_name, b.third_category_code, b.total_time, b.handle_code, b.handle_name, b.handle_date, b.handle_dept_code, b.handle_dept_name, b.urgent_level, b.if_reply, b.reply_time, b.press_num, b.hope_time, b.end_time, b.warning_flag, b.handle_position_code, b.handle_position_name, b.certificate_upload_status FROM ACT_RU_TASK t LEFT JOIN tb_bpm_business b ON t.PROC_INST_ID_ = b.process_instance_id LEFT JOIN TB_BPM_BUSINESS_SEARCH c ON b.business_id = c.id WHERE t.entrust_people_code = :2 AND b.business_status = 1 AND t.task_status = 1 UNION SELECT 5 AS flag, t.ID_ AS taskId, NVL(t.entrust_people_code, t.locker_code) A S lockerCode, NVL(t.entrust_people_name, t.locker_name) AS lockerName, t.TASK_DEF_KEY_ AS nodeId, c.complainType, c.complainTend, c.callNumber, b.business_id, b.business_name, b.business_status, b.temp_id, b.temp_version, b.process_id, b.process_version, b.process_instance_id, b.first_category_name, b.first_category_code, b.second_category_name, b.second_category_code, b.third_category_name, b.third_category_code, b.total_time, b.handle_code, b.handle_name, b.handle_date, b.handle_dept_code, b.handle_dept_name, b.urgent_level, b.if_reply, b.reply_time, b.press_num, b.hope_time, b.end_time, b.warning_flag, b.handle_position_code, b.handle_position_name, b.certificate_upload_status FROM ACT_RU_TASK t LEFT JOIN tb_bpm_business b ON t.PROC_INST_ID_ = b.process_instance_id LEFT JOIN TB_BPM_BUSINESS_SEARCH c ON b.business_id = c.id WHERE t.locker_code = :3 AND t.task_status = 1 AND b.business_status = 1 AND t.entrust_people_code IS NULL AND t.ASSIGNEE_ IS NOT NULL) b LEFT JOIN tb_bpm_relate tbr ON b.business_id = tbr.business_id LEFT JOIN tb_core_summary_record tcsr ON b.callNumber = tcsr.inboundno AND tcsr.calltype = '0' GROUP BY b.flag, b.taskId, b.lockerCode, b.lockerName, b.nodeId, b.complainType, b.complainTend, b.callNumber, b.business_id, b.business_name, b.business_status, b.temp_id, b.temp_version, b.process_id, b.process_version, b.handle_position_name, b.certificate_upload_status) table_count

5hm8u9mu2gga2SELECT count(id) as countNum, service_name FROM tb_bpm_info WHERE (to_user = :1 or to_user = :2 ) and is_read =0 group by service_name

5rr0n38hh0x2c SELECT DISTINCT a.handle_date AS handleDate , a.process_instance_id AS processInstanceId , CASE WHEN d.business_id IS NOT NULL THEN '8' when t.locker_code is not null then '3' ELSE to_char(a.business_status) END AS businessStatus , CASE WHEN a.certificate_upload_status IN ('1' , '3') THEN '1' ELSE '0' END AS certificateFlag , CASE WHEN b.business_id IS NOT NULL THEN '0' WHEN d.business_id IS NOT NULL or a.business_status ='8' then '0' ELSE '1' END AS newRequestFlag, (CASE WHEN c.TYPE IS NOT NULL THEN (select summary_name from tb_core_callin_summary where summary_id = to_number(substr(c.type, instr(c.type, '[')+1, instr(c.type, ', ')-instr(c.type, '[')-1))) else null end || '-' || CASE WHEN c.TYPE IS NOT NULL THEN (select summary_name from tb_core_callin_summary where summary_id = to_number(substr(c.type, instr(c.type, ', ', -1)+2, instr(c.type, ']')-instr(c.type, ', ', -1)-2))) else null end ) AS businessType FROM TB_ BPM_BUSINESS a LEFT JOIN ACT_RU_TASK t on t.PROC_INST_ID_ = a.process_instance_id LEFT JOIN tb_bpm_flow b ON a.business_id = b.business_id AND b.flow_type = '11' AND b.create_id = 'kehu' LEFT JOIN tb_bpm_flow d ON a.business_id = d.business_id AND d.flow_type = '12' LEFT JOIN tb_bpm_business_search c ON a.business_id = c.id where a.business_name in ('????' , '??????') and ( c.callNumber = :1 or c.cardId = :2 ) and to_char(a.handle_date, 'yyyy-MM-dd HH24:mm:ss') >= :3 and a.business_status in ('1' , '3', '8') ORDER BY a.handle_date desc

7n9pz49qw4f39select decode(upper(failover_method), NULL, 0 , 'BASIC', 1, 'PRECONNECT', 2 , 'PREPARSE', 4 , 0), decode(upper(failover_type), NULL, 1, 'NONE', 1, 'SESSION', 2, 'SELECT', 4, 'TRANSACTION', 8, 1), failover_retries, failover_delay, flags, nvl(replay_initiation_timeout, 300), decode(upper(session_state_consistency), 'STATIC', 16, 0), failover_restore from sys.service$ where name = :1
7nz40322ac7z6 SELECT count(0) FROM (SELECT b.flag, b.taskId, b.lockerCode, b.lockerName, b.nodeId, b.complainType, b.complainTend, b.callNumber, count(tbr.business_id) relation, count(inboundno) phoneCount, b.business_id, b.business_name, b.business_status, b.temp_id, b.temp_version, b.process_id, b.process_version, b.process_instance_id, b.first_category_name, b.first_category_code, b.second_category_name, b.second_category_code, b.third_category_name, b.third_category_code, b.total_time, b.handle_code, b.handle_name, b.handle_date, b.handle_dept_code, b.handle_dept_name, b.urgent_level, b.if_reply, b.reply_time, b.press_num, b.hope_time, b.end_time, b.warning_flag, b.handle_position_code, b.handle_position_name, b.certificate_upload_status FROM (SELECT 2 AS flag, t.ID_ AS taskId, NVL(t.entrust_people_code, t.locker_code) AS lockerCode, NVL(t.entrust_people_name, t.locker_name) AS lockerName, t.TASK_DEF_KEY_ AS nodeId, c.complainType, c.complainTend, c.callNumber, b.business_id, b.business_name, b.business_status, b.temp_id, b.temp_version, b.process_id, b.process_version, b.process_instance_id, b.first_category_name, b.first_category_code, b.second_category_name, b.second_category_code, b.third_category_name, b.third_category_code, b.total_time, b.handle_code, b.handle_name, b.handle_date, b.handle_dept_code, b.handle_dept_name, b.urgent_level, b.if_reply, b.reply_time, b.press_num, b.hope_time, b.end_time, b.warning_flag, b.handle_position_code, b.handle_position_name, b.certificate_upload_status FROM ACT_RU_TASK t LEFT JOIN tb_bpm_business b ON t.PROC_INST_ID_ = b.process_instance_id LEFT JOIN ACT_RU_IDENTITYLINK i ON i.TASK_ID_ = t.ID_ LEFT JOIN TB_BPM_BUSINESS_SEARCH c ON b.business_id = c.id WHERE (t.locker_code IS NULL OR t.locker_code = '') AND b.business_status = 1 AND (t.task_status = 0 OR t.task_status IS NULL) AND t.entrust_people_code IS NULL AND t.ASSIGNEE_ = :1 AND b.handle_dept_code = :2 AND (i.GROUP_ID_ IS NULL OR i.GROUP_ID_ = '') UNION SELECT 3 AS flag, t.ID_ AS taskId, NVL(t.entrust_people_code, t.locker_code) AS lockerCode, NVL(t.entrust_people_name, t.locker_name) AS lockerName, t.TASK_DEF_KEY_ AS nodeId, c.complainType, c.complainTend, c.callNumber, b.business_id, b.business_name, b.business_status, b.temp_id, b.temp_version, b.process_id, b.process_version, b.process_instance_id, b.first_category_name, b.first_category_code, b.second_category_name, b.second_category_code, b.third_category_name, b.third_category_code, b.total_time, b.handle_code, b.handle_name, b.handle_date, b.handle_dept_code, b.handle_dept_name, b.urgent_level, b.if_reply, b.reply_time, b.press_num, b.hope_time, b.end_time, b.warning_flag, b.handle_position_code, b.handle_position_name, b.certificate_upload_status FROM ACT_RU_TASK t LEFT JOIN tb_bpm_business b ON t.PROC_INST_ID_ = b.process_instance_id LEFT JOIN ACT_RU_IDENTITYLINK i ON i.TASK_ID_ = t.ID_ LEFT JOIN TB_BPM_BUSINESS_SEARCH c ON b.business_id = c.id WHERE (t.locker_code IS NULL OR t.locker_code = '') AND b.business_status = 1 AND (t.task_status = 0 OR t.task_status IS NULL) AND t.entrust_people_code IS NULL AND t.ASSIGNEE_ = :3 AND i.GROUP_ID_ = :4 ) b LEFT JOIN tb_bpm_relate tbr ON b.business_id = tbr.business_id LEFT JOIN tb_core_summary_record tcsr ON b.callNumber = tcsr.inboundno AND tcsr.calltype = '0' GROUP BY b.flag, b.taskId, b.lockerCode, b.lockerName, b.nodeId, b.complainType, b.complainTend, b.callNumber, b.business_id, b.business_name, b.business_status, b.temp_id, b.temp_version, b.process_id, b.process_version, b.process_instance_id, b.first_category_name, b.first_category_code, b.second_category_name, b.second_category_code, b.third_category_name, b.third_category_code, b.total_time, b.handle_code, b.handle_name, b.handle_date, b.handle_dept_code, b.handle_dept_name, b.urgent_level, b.if_reply, b.reply_time, b.press_num, b.hope_time, b.end_time, b.warning_flag, b.handle_position_code, b.handle_position_name, b.certificate_upload_status) table_count
7p3vn53bsctw9 SELECT count(0) FROM (SELECT b.flag, b.taskId, b.lockerCode, b.lockerName, b.nodeId, b.complainType, b.complainTend, b.callNumber, count(tbr.business_id) relation, count(inboundno) phoneCount, b.business_id, b.business_name, b.business_status, b.temp_id, b.temp_version, b.process_id, b.process_version, b.process_instance_id, b.first_category_name, b.first_category_code, b.second_category_name, b.second_category_code, b.third_category_name, b.third_category_code, b.total_time, b.handle_code, b.handle_name, b.handle_date, b.handle_dept_code, b.handle_dept_name, b.urgent_level, b.if_reply, b.reply_time, b.press_num, b.hope_time, b.end_time, b.warning_flag, b.handle_position_code, b.handle_position_name, b.certificate_upload_status FROM (SELECT 1 AS flag, t.ID_ AS taskId, NVL(t.entrust_people_code, t.locker_code) AS lockerCode, NVL(t.entrust_people_name, t.locker_name) AS lockerName, t.TASK_DEF_KEY_ AS nodeId, c.complainType, c.complainTend, c.callNumber, b.business_id, b.business_name, b.business_status, b.temp_id, b.temp_version, b.process_id, b.process_version, b.process_instance_id, b.first_category_name, b.first_category_code, b.second_category_name, b.second_category_code, b.third_category_name, b.third_category_code, b.total_time, b.handle_code, b.handle_name, b.handle_date, b.handle_dept_code, b.handle_dept_name, b.urgent_level, b.if_reply, b.reply_time, b.press_num, b.hope_time, b.end_time, b.warning_flag, b.handle_position_code, b.handle_position_name, b.certificate_upload_status FROM ACT_RU_TASK t LEFT JOIN tb_bpm_business b ON t.PROC_INST_ID_ = b.process_instance_id LEFT JOIN TB_BPM_BUSINESS_SEARCH c ON b.business_id = c.id WHERE t.locker_code = :1 AND t.task_status = 1 AND b.business_status = 1 AND t.entrust_people_code IS NULL AND t.ASSIGNEE_ IS NULL AND c.cardId LIKE :2 UNION SELECT 4 AS flag, t.ID_ AS taskId, NVL(t.entrust_people_code, t.locker_code) AS lockerCode, NVL(t. entrust_people_name, t.locker_name) AS lockerName, t.TASK_DEF_KEY_ AS nodeId, c.complainType, c.complainTend, c.callNumber, b.business_id, b.business_name, b.business_status, b.temp_id, b.temp_version, b.process_id, b.process_version, b.process_instance_id, b.first_category_name, b.first_category_code, b.second_category_name, b.second_category_code, b.third_category_name, b.third_category_code, b.total_time, b.handle_code, b.handle_name, b.handle_date, b.handle_dept_code, b.handle_dept_name, b.urgent_level, b.if_reply, b.reply_time, b.press_num, b.hope_time, b.end_time, b.warning_flag, b.handle_position_code, b.handle_position_name, b.certificate_upload_status FROM ACT_RU_TASK t LEFT JOIN tb_bpm_business b ON t.PROC_INST_ID_ = b.process_instance_id LEFT JOIN TB_BPM_BUSINESS_SEARCH c ON b.business_id = c.id WHERE t.entrust_people_code = :3 AND b.business_status = 1 AND t.task_status = 1 AND c.cardId LIKE :4 UNION SELECT 5 AS flag, t.ID_ AS taskId, NVL(t.entrust_people_code, t.locker_code) AS lockerCode, NVL(t.entrust_people_name, t.locker_name) AS lockerName, t.TASK_DEF_KEY_ AS nodeId, c.complainType, c.complainTend, c.callNumber, b.business_id, b.business_name, b.business_status, b.temp_id, b.temp_version, b.process_id, b.process_version, b.process_instance_id, b.first_category_name, b.first_category_code, b.second_category_name, b.second_category_code, b.third_category_name, b.third_category_code, b.total_time, b.handle_code, b.handle_name, b.handle_date, b.handle_dept_code, b.handle_dept_name, b.urgent_level, b.if_reply, b.reply_time, b.press_num, b.hope_time, b.end_time, b.warning_flag, b.handle_position_code, b.handle_position_name, b.certificate_upload_status FROM ACT_RU_TASK t LEFT JOIN tb_bpm_business b ON t.PROC_INST_ID_ = b.process_instance_id LEFT JOIN TB_BPM_BUSINESS_SEARCH c ON b.business_id = c.id WHERE t.locker_code = :5 AND t.task_status = 1 AND b.business_status = 1 AND t.en trust_people_code IS NULL AND t.ASSIGNEE_ IS NOT NULL AND c.cardId LIKE :6 ) b LEFT JOIN tb_bpm_relate tbr ON b.business_id = tbr.business_id LEFT JOIN tb_core_summary_record tcsr ON b.callNumber = tcsr.inboundno AND tcsr.calltype = '0' GROUP BY b.flag, b.taskId, b.lockerCode, b.lockerName, b.nodeId, b.complainType, b.complainTend, b.callNumber, b.business_id, b.business_name, b.business_status, b.temp_id, b.temp_version, b.process_id, b.process_version, b.process_instance_id, b.first_category_name, b.first_category_code, b.second_category_name, b.second_category_code, b.third_category_name, b.third_category_code, b.total_time, b.handle_code, b.handle_name, b.handle_date, b.handle_dept_code, b.handle_dept_name, b.urgent_level, b.if_reply, b.reply_time, b.press_num, b.hope_time, b.end_time, b.warning_flag, b.handle_position_code, b.handle_position_name, b.certificate_upload_status) table_count
84jvuqxykyxs4INSERT INTO T_DINGDING_FLOWING ( F_TYPE, F_ESBNO, F_SERIALNO, F_DATA, F_DATA_ALL ) VALUES ( :1 , :2 , :3 , :4 , :5 ) RETURNING ID INTO :6

865qwpcdyggkkselect spare6 from user$ where user#=:1
8addc68xcrgq5 SELECT * FROM ( SELECT TABLES.CON_ID, SYSDATE END_TIME, TABLES.OWNER, TABLES.TABLE_NAME, PARTITION_NAME, SUBPARTITION_NAME, ROUND((DELETES + UPDATES + INSERTS) / NUM_ROWS * 100) PERCENTAGE, TABLES.LAST_ANALYZED, TABLES.NUM_ROWS, DELETES, UPDATES, INSERTS from cdb_tables tables, cdb_tab_modifications modifications where tables.owner = modifications.table_owner AND tables.con_id = modifications.con_id and tables.table_name = modifications.table_name and num_rows > 10000 and modifications.PARTITION_NAME is null and modifications.SUBPARTITION_NAME is null and round((deletes + updates + inserts) / num_rows * 100) >= 10 and tables.owner not in ('SYS', 'SYSTEM') union all SELECT TABLES.CON_ID, SYSDATE END_TIME, TABLES.table_owner, TABLES.TABLE_NAME, tables.PARTITION_NAME, SUBPARTITION_NAME, ROUND((DELETES + UPDATES + INSERTS) / NUM_ROWS * 100) PERCENTAGE, TABLES.LAST_ANALY ZED, TABLES.NUM_ROWS, DELETES, UPDATES, INSERTS from cdb_tab_partitions tables, cdb_tab_modifications modifications where tables.table_owner = modifications.table_owner AND tables.con_id = modifications.con_id and tables.table_name = modifications.table_name and tables.PARTITION_NAME=modifications.PARTITION_NAME and num_rows > 10000 and modifications.PARTITION_NAME is not null and modifications.SUBPARTITION_NAME is null and round((deletes + updates + inserts) / num_rows * 100) >= 10 and tables.table_owner not in ('SYS', 'SYSTEM') union all SELECT TABLES.CON_ID, SYSDATE END_TIME, TABLES.table_owner, TABLES.TABLE_NAME, tables.PARTITION_NAME, tables.SUBPARTITION_NAME, ROUND((DELETES + UPDATES + INSERTS) / NUM_ROWS * 100) PERCENTAGE, TABLES.LAST_ANALYZED, TABLES.NUM_ROWS, DELETES, UPDATES, INSERTS from cdb_tab_subpartitions tables, cdb_tab_modifications modifications where tables. table_owner = modifications.table_owner AND tables.con_id = modifications.con_id and tables.table_name = modifications.table_name and tables.partition_name=modifications.partition_name and tables.subpartition_name=modifications.subpartition_name and num_rows > 10000 and modifications.SUBPARTITION_NAME is not null and round((deletes + updates + inserts) / num_rows * 100) >= 10 and tables.table_owner not in ('SYS', 'SYSTEM') order by PERCENTAGE DESC ) where rownum<=50

9pm4rmyragkdg SELECT * FROM ( SELECT TMP_PAGE.*, ROWNUM ROW_ID FROM ( SELECT t.id, t.custid, t.callid, t.inboundno, t.custname, t.inboundarea, t.starttime, t.endtime, t.ivrpress, t.ivrdata, t.handuptype, t.istransfor, t.satisfaction, t.channel, t.record_field2, t.record_field3, t.record_field4, t.record_field5, t.remark, t.createtime, t.agentid, t.idno, t.redialcustnum, t.outboundno, t.extension, t.reservednum, t.talktime, t.bankidno, t.certificatetype, t.ncardno, t.custtype, t.custno, t.service _code, t.service_content, t.calltype, t.disflag, t.m_returnmsg, t.m_returnmsgtype, t.evaluate, t.wishcode, t.outstate, t.unsatisreason, t.unsatisremark, t.activitydataid, t.stscflag, t.serviceid, t.ctino, t.record_file, t.sex, t.businesstype, t.businessname, t.user_bank_address, t.account_bank_address, t.video_track, t.business_imgs, t.guard_card, t.pass_flag, t.pass_code, t.video_manner, t.backtracking, t.todaydate, t.custcharacter, t.markcapa, t.file_path, t.message_box, t.call_time, t.jobs_call_list_id, t.external_number, t.history_identity historyIdentity, u.user_name, ( case when t.inboundno is not null then t.inboundno when t.outboundno is not null then t.outboundno end ) telphone FROM tb_core_summary_record t left join tb_core_users u on u.job_num = t.agentid WHERE t.agentid = :1 and t.starttime >= :2 and :3 >= t.endtime order by t.starttime desc ) TMP_PAGE) WHERE ROW_ID <= :4 AND ROW_ID > :5

d4dutjhmar6c0 SELECT * FROM ( SELECT TMP_PAGE.*, ROWNUM ROW_ID FROM ( SELECT t.id, t.custid, t.callid, t.inboundno, t.custname, t.inboundarea, t.starttime, t.endtime, t.ivrpress, t.ivrdata, t.handuptype, t.istransfor, t.satisfaction, t.channel, t.record_field2, t.record_field3, t.record_field4, t.record_field5, t.remark, t.createtime, t.agentid, t.idno, t.redialcustnum, t.outboundno, t.extension, t.reservednum, t.talktime, t.bankidno, t.certificatetype, t.ncardno, t.custtype, t.custno, t.service _code, t.service_content, t.calltype, t.disflag, t.m_returnmsg, t.m_returnmsgtype, t.evaluate, t.wishcode, t.outstate, t.unsatisreason, t.unsatisremark, t.activitydataid, t.stscflag, t.serviceid, t.ctino, t.record_file, t.sex, t.businesstype, t.businessname, t.user_bank_address, t.account_bank_address, t.video_track, t.business_imgs, t.guard_card, t.pass_flag, t.pass_code, t.video_manner, t.backtracking, t.todaydate, t.custcharacter, t.markcapa, t.file_path, t.message_box, t.call_time, t.jobs_call_list_id, t.external_number, t.history_identity historyIdentity, u.user_name, ( case when t.inboundno is not null then t.inboundno when t.outboundno is not null then t.outboundno end ) telphone FROM tb_core_summary_record t left join tb_core_users u on u.job_num = t.agentid WHERE t.inboundno like :1 order by t.starttime desc ) TMP_PAGE) WHERE ROW_ID <= :2 AND ROW_ID > :3

PGA Aggr Target Stats

  • B: Begin Snap E: End Snap (rows dentified with B or E contain data which is absolute i.e. not diffed over the interval)
  • Auto PGA Target - actual workarea memory target
  • W/A PGA Used - amount of memory used for all Workareas (manual + auto)
  • %PGA W/A Mem - percentage of PGA memory allocated to workareas
  • %Auto W/A Mem - percentage of workarea memory controlled by Auto Mem Mgmt
  • %Man W/A Mem - percentage of workarea memory under manual control

Shared Pool Advisory

  • SP: Shared Pool Est LC: Estimated Library Cache Factr: Factor
  • Note there is often a 1:Many correlation between a single logical object in the Library Cache, and the physical number of memory objects associated with it. Therefore comparing the number of Lib Cache objects (e.g. in v$librarycache), with the number of Lib Cache Memory Objects is invalid.

Undo Segment Summary

  • Min/Max TR (mins) - Min and Max Tuned Retention (minutes)

  • eS - expired Stolen, eR - expired Released, eU - expired reUsed

Active Session History (ASH) Report

  • Top SQL with Top Events

Back to Top

Top SQL with Top Events

  • Top SQL statements by DB Time along with the top events by DB Time for those SQLs.
  • % Activity is the percentage of DB Time due to the SQL.
  • % Event is the percentage of DB Time due to the event that the SQL is waiting on.
  • % Row Source is the percentage of DB Time due to the row source for the SQL waiting on the event.
  • Executions is the number of executions of the SQL that were sampled in ASH.
SQL IDPlan HashExecutions% ActivityEvent% EventTop Row Source% Row SourceSQL Text

Back to Top

Top SQL with Top Row Sources

  • Top SQL statements by DB Time along with the top row sources by DB Time for those SQLs.
  • % Activity is the percentage of DB Time due to the SQL.
  • % Row Source is the percentage of DB Time spent on the row source by that SQL.
  • % Event is the percentage of DB Time spent on the event by the SQL executing the row source.
  • Executions is the number of executions of the SQL that were sampled in ASH.

Top Sessions

  • '# Samples Active' shows the number of ASH samples in which the session was found waiting for that particular event. The percentage shown in this column is calculated with respect to wall clock time and not total database activity.
  • 'XIDs' shows the number of distinct transaction IDs sampled in ASH when the session was waiting for that particular event

Top Blocking Sessions

  • Blocking session activity percentages are calculated with respect to waits on enqueues, latches and "buffer busy" only
  • '% Activity' represents the load on the database caused by a particular blocking session
  • '# Samples Active' shows the number of ASH samples in which the blocking session was found active.
  • 'XIDs' shows the number of distinct transaction IDs sampled in ASH when the blocking session was found active.

Back to Top

ADDM Task ADDM:2668322570_1_19322

          ADDM Report for Task 'ADDM:2668322570_1_19322'
          ----------------------------------------------

Analysis Period
---------------
AWR snapshot range from 19321 to 19322.
Time period starts at 02-FEB-26 12.00.25 PM
Time period ends at 02-FEB-26 01.00.35 PM

Analysis Target
---------------
Database 'ORACC' with DB ID 2668322570.
Database version 12.2.0.1.0.
ADDM performed an analysis of instance oracc, numbered 1 and hosted at
npkkf231.

Activity During the Analysis Period
-----------------------------------
Total database time was 40994 seconds.
The average number of active sessions was 11.36.

Summary of Findings
-------------------
   Description                     Active Sessions      Recommendations
                                   Percent of Activity
   ------------------------------  -------------------  ---------------
1  Shared Pool Latches             7.12 | 62.66         0
2  Session Connect and Disconnect  2.02 | 17.8          1
3  Top SQL Statements              1.91 | 16.8          1
4  Soft Parse                      .86 | 7.53           2
5  Unusual "Other" Wait Event      .24 | 2.13           3


~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~


          Findings and Recommendations
          ----------------------------

Finding 1: Shared Pool Latches
Impact is 7.12 active sessions, 62.66% of total activity.
---------------------------------------------------------
Contention for latches related to the shared pool was consuming significant
database time.
Waits for "library cache: mutex X" amounted to 13% of database time.
Waits for "latch: shared pool" amounted to 43% of database time.
Waits for "cursor: mutex S" amounted to 4% of database time.

   No recommendations are available.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Wait class "Concurrency" was consuming significant database time.
      Impact is 7.26 active sessions, 63.95% of total activity.


Finding 2: Session Connect and Disconnect
Impact is 2.02 active sessions, 17.8% of total activity.
--------------------------------------------------------
Session connect and disconnect calls were consuming significant database time.

   Recommendation 1: Application Analysis
   Estimated benefit is 2.02 active sessions, 17.8% of total activity.
   -------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Investigate application logic for possible reduction of connect and
      disconnect calls. For example, you might use a connection pool scheme in
      the middle tier.


Finding 3: Top SQL Statements
Impact is 1.91 active sessions, 16.8% of total activity.
--------------------------------------------------------
SQL statements consuming significant database time were found. These
statements offer a good opportunity for performance improvement.

   Recommendation 1: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is 1.91 active sessions, 16.8% of total activity.
   -------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Run SQL Tuning Advisor on the SELECT statement with SQL_ID
      "0bckxtm13v9sd". Additionally, investigate this statement for possible
      performance improvements. You can supplement the information given here
      with an ASH report for this SQL_ID.
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID 0bckxtm13v9sd.
         SELECT id,
         custid,
         callid,
         inboundno,
         custname,
         inboundarea,
         starttime,
         endtime,
         ivrpress,
         ivrdata,
         handuptype,
         istransfor,
         satisfaction,
         channel,
         record_field2,
         record_field3,
         record_field4,
         record_field5,
         remark,
         createtime,
         agentid,
         idno,
         redialcustnum,
         outboundno,
         extension,
         reservednum,
         talktime,
         bankidno,
         certificatetype,
         ncardno,
         custtype,
         custno,
         service_code,
         service_content,
         calltype,
         disflag,
         m_returnmsg,
         m_returnmsgtype,
         evaluate,
         wishcode,
         outstate,
         unsatisreason,
         unsatisremark,
         activitydataid,
         stscflag,
         serviceid,
         ctino,
         record_file,
         sex,
         businesstype,
         businessname,
         user_bank_address,
         account_bank_address,
         video_track,
         business_imgs,
         guard_card,
         pass_flag,
         pass_code,
         video_manner,
         backtracking,
         todaydate,
         custcharacter,
         markcapa,
         file_path,
         message_box,
         call_time,
         jobs_call_list_id,
         external_number
         FROM tb_core_summary_record
         WHERE endtime <=  to_date(:1 ,'yyyy-mm-dd HH24:MI:SS ') + 1
         AND endtime> to_date(:2 ,'yyyy-mm-dd HH24:MI:SS ')
         and agentid = :3
         and satisfaction = :4
   Rationale
      The SQL spent 30% of its database time on CPU, I/O and Cluster waits.
      This part of database time may be improved by the SQL Tuning Advisor.
      Look at data given below and an ASH report for further performance
      improvements.
   Rationale
      Database time for this SQL was divided as follows: 66% for SQL
      execution, 34% for parsing, 0% for PL/SQL execution and 0% for Java
      execution.
   Rationale
      Waiting for event "cursor: mutex S" in wait class "Concurrency"
      accounted for 34% of the database time spent in processing the SQL
      statement with SQL_ID "0bckxtm13v9sd".


Finding 4: Soft Parse
Impact is .86 active sessions, 7.53% of total activity.
-------------------------------------------------------
Soft parsing of SQL statements was consuming significant database time.

   Recommendation 1: Application Analysis
   Estimated benefit is .86 active sessions, 7.53% of total activity.
   ------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Investigate application logic to keep open the frequently used cursors.
      Note that cursors are closed by both cursor close calls and session
      disconnects.

   Recommendation 2: Database Configuration
   Estimated benefit is .86 active sessions, 7.53% of total activity.
   ------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Consider increasing the session cursor cache size by increasing the
      value of parameter "session_cached_cursors".
   Rationale
      The value of parameter "session_cached_cursors" was "50" during the
      analysis period.


Finding 5: Unusual "Other" Wait Event
Impact is .24 active sessions, 2.13% of total activity.
-------------------------------------------------------
Wait event "PX Deq: Join ACK" in wait class "Other" was consuming significant
database time.

   Recommendation 1: Application Analysis
   Estimated benefit is .24 active sessions, 2.13% of total activity.
   ------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Investigate the cause for high "PX Deq: Join ACK" waits. Refer to
      Oracle's "Database Reference" for the description of this wait event.

   Recommendation 2: Application Analysis
   Estimated benefit is .24 active sessions, 2.13% of total activity.
   ------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Investigate the cause for high "PX Deq: Join ACK" waits in Module "JDBC
      Thin Client".

   Recommendation 3: Application Analysis
   Estimated benefit is .24 active sessions, 2.13% of total activity.
   ------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Investigate the cause for high "PX Deq: Join ACK" waits in Service
      "oracc".

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Wait class "Other" was consuming significant database time.
      Impact is .39 active sessions, 3.41% of total activity.

          Additional Information
          ----------------------

Miscellaneous Information
-------------------------
Wait class "Application" was not consuming significant database time.
Wait class "Commit" was not consuming significant database time.
Wait class "Configuration" was not consuming significant database time.
CPU was not a bottleneck for the instance.
Wait class "Network" was not consuming significant database time.
Wait class "User I/O" was not consuming significant database time.
Hard parsing of SQL statements was not consuming significant database time.
